---
title: "ggnomics: Highest-Expressed Genes"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import importlib.util, os
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("mock_data", os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "examples", "00_mock_data.py"))
mock_data = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mock_data)

from ggnomics import plot_highest_exprs

df    = mock_data.make_mock_df(n_cells=500)
adata = mock_data.make_mock_anndata(n_cells=500)
sce   = mock_data.make_mock_sce(n_cells=500)
```

## Overview

`plot_highest_exprs` identifies the top-``n`` most highly expressed genes
(by median fraction of total library size) and shows their per-cell
distribution as horizontal boxplots.  This is a standard quality control
plot — the top spots should be occupied by housekeeping genes or ribosomal
genes, not marker genes.

---

## 1 — Top 20 genes, DataFrame

```{python}
p = plot_highest_exprs(df, n=20, title="Top 20 highest expressed genes")
p.save("examples/img/05a_highest_df.png", width=7, height=8, dpi=120)
p
```

## 2 — Colour boxes by cluster

```{python}
p = plot_highest_exprs(df, n=20, color_cells_by="cluster",
                       title="Top 20 genes — coloured by cluster")
p.save("examples/img/05b_highest_cluster.png", width=9, height=8, dpi=120)
p
```

## 3 — AnnData (logcounts layer)

```{python}
p = plot_highest_exprs(adata, n=20, layer="logcounts",
                       title="AnnData — top 20 genes (logcounts)")
p.save("examples/img/05c_highest_adata.png", width=7, height=8, dpi=120)
p
```

## 4 — SCE

```{python}
p = plot_highest_exprs(sce, n=20, title="SCE — top 20 genes")
p.save("examples/img/05d_highest_sce.png", width=7, height=8, dpi=120)
p
```

---

## Key parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `n` | Number of top genes to show |
| `layer` | AnnData layer / SCE assay for expression |
| `color_cells_by` | Obs column to colour box fill |
| `palette` | `{category: hex}` color mapping |
